waterTable.evidenceSnippet: Groundwater encountered at 4.5 feet. Site is adjacent to tidal marshland and water levels may fluctuate.
Free water at 5.0 feet below existing grade in Boring B-2.
Shallow groundwater at 3.5 feet, consistent with proximity to wetland area.
Water table at 6 feet in B-4, slightly deeper than eastern borings.
Groundwater at 4 feet with artesian-like conditions observed.
Free water at 5.5 feet below ground surface.
Very shallow water at 3 feet. Seasonal high water table expected at or near surface.
Groundwater at 4.5 feet consistent with site hydrology.
Water observed at 5 feet during drilling of Boring B-9.
Shallow groundwater at 3.5 feet near site boundary.

borings.notes: Organic clay layer from 0-4 feet. Very soft, highly compressible. Not suitable for direct support.
Organic silt in upper 3 feet. Fat clay below with PI of 42.
Peat layer from 0-3 feet. Complete removal required. Unsuitable for any structural support.
Better conditions than eastern borings. Dense sand below 15 feet provides good bearing.
Organic clay layer to 5 feet. Soft clay zone from 5-12 feet.
Marginally adequate upper soils. Improvement recommended.
Most challenging boring. Organic and fatty soils to 8 feet depth.
Moderate conditions. Soft clay zone from 3-8 feet.
Relatively favorable conditions in western portion of site.
Organic layer near surface. Ground improvement needed.

slab.summary: Structural slab on drilled piers required. Slab-on-grade NOT recommended due to organic and compressible soils.
If ground-supported slab is used, minimum 36-inch surcharge and preloading program required prior to construction.

foundation.summary: Deep foundations (drilled piers or driven piles) required. Minimum penetration to dense sand layer below 20 feet.
Negative skin friction (downdrag) must be accounted for in pile design due to consolidating organic soils.

pavement.summary: Complete removal of organic and peat layers required under all pavement areas. Minimum 3-foot over-excavation.
Geotextile fabric separator required between subgrade and aggregate base course throughout parking and drive areas.

riskFlags.evidence: Site is adjacent to tidal marshland. Organic soils and peat layers found in multiple borings.
riskFlags.description: Marshy conditions create extreme foundation challenges. Ground improvement or deep foundations essential.

riskFlags.evidence: Groundwater at 3-6 feet across entire site. Seasonal high water table may be at surface level.
riskFlags.description: Extensive dewatering system required for all excavations. Permanent drainage consideration needed.

riskFlags.evidence: Organic and peat layers from 0-5 feet require complete removal and replacement across 70% of site.
riskFlags.description: Massive over-excavation costs anticipated. Budget for removal and replacement of organic soils.

riskFlags.evidence: Fat clay (CH) with PI values up to 42 found in multiple borings below the organic layer.
riskFlags.description: Fatty soils compound foundation challenges. Swell/shrink behavior creates additional risk.

riskFlags.evidence: Shallow water table and organic soils create persistent moisture challenges across the site.
riskFlags.description: Comprehensive moisture management plan required for construction and long-term performance.

riskFlags.evidence: Pavement subgrade entirely unsuitable without extensive over-excavation and replacement.
riskFlags.description: Full undercut of organic soils required under all paved areas. Minimum 3-foot depth.

chatResponses.response: Groundwater is extremely shallow at this site, ranging from 3 to 6 feet. This is a major construction challenge requiring extensive dewatering.
Deep foundations (drilled piers or driven piles) are mandatory due to organic and compressible soils. Shallow foundations are NOT suitable at this site.
This is a high-risk site (score: 88). Major concerns include marshy conditions, very shallow groundwater, extensive organic soils requiring removal, and fatty clay layers.
